title: Slow-cooker beef brisket
subtitle: Low and slow until the grain pulls apart
excerpt: Beef brisket rubbed with smoked paprika and cooked for eight to nine hours until it shreds along the grain with two forks. The long cook breaks down the collagen and fat throughout the flat and point, producing a yielding, deeply savoury result that carved brisket cannot match.
sourceNotes: Slow-cooked brisket is a cross between the American BBQ tradition and British pot-roast cooking. The slow cooker version became popular in British home kitchens as an accessible way to achieve the falling-apart pulled beef that would otherwise require a smoker or a full oven braise. The muscle runs in one direction — pull with the grain for long strands, against it for chunks.

recipe.freezeNotes: Pull, cool, and freeze in portions. Reheat with a splash of the cooking juices.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Brisket is better made a day ahead. Refrigerate in its juices and reheat covered in the oven at 160°C for 30 minutes.

content.text: Brisket has two muscles: the flat (leaner) and the point (fattier). The point pulls apart more richly. If you can only find the flat, the result is still good but slightly less yielding. Either way, 8 hours is the minimum — under-braise it and the texture is chewy rather than falling.

Mix the {{smoked-paprika}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} together and rub all over the {{beef-brisket}}. Scatter the {{onion}} and {{garlic}} across the base of the slow cooker. Place the brisket on top. Stir the {{tomato-puree}} and {{worcestershire-sauce}} into the {{stock-beef}} and pour around the beef. Tuck in the {{bay-leaves}}. Cook on low for 8 to 9 hours, until the meat shreds with two forks along the grain. Rest for 15 minutes before pulling apart. Skim the fat from the cooking juices and reduce in a small saucepan if a thicker sauce is wanted.

items.symptom: Brisket chewy and resistant after 8 hours
items.cause: Cooker running cool, or brisket thicker than average
items.fix: Add another hour on low or switch to high for the final 2 hours. Brisket is impossible to overcook — it will only get more tender with time

items.cause: Brisket point has a high fat content
items.fix: Cool the finished dish overnight, then lift off the solidified fat cap before reheating. The result is a cleaner, richer sauce

items.cause: No browning before the slow cooker
items.fix: Sear the brisket in a very hot pan for 3 to 4 minutes per side before adding to the slow cooker. Not essential, but it adds colour and a slight crust

content.text: Slow-cooked brisket works for Sunday dinners, sandwiches the following day, and as a filling for jacket potatoes. The pulled texture is more versatile than sliced beef. It is the cheapest roasting cut and among the most rewarding when given the time it needs.
